SDA's deliberate pace shift prioritizes operational maturity over launch cadence, signaling that constellation viability depends more on network integration than raw satellite numbers.

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

Op-Ed: Repeated government shutdowns normalize aviation instability

U.S. Government shutdowns disrupt airport operations and a variety of private businesses, from airlines and bizjet operators to airport shops and restaurants. Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Mohamed Yousuf, the CEO and founder of Smart Workforce AI. When government shutdowns occur, they...

Recurring shutdowns erode operational predictability across aviation's ecosystem, creating systemic inefficiencies that extend beyond federal agencies to private operators and supply chains.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA halts work on Gateway to develop a lunar base

NASA is halting plans to develop the lunar Gateway and instead focusing on the development of a lunar base. The post NASA halts work on Gateway to develop a lunar base appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's pivot from Gateway to direct lunar base development could accelerate surface operations but may complicate logistics coordination with international partners already invested in the orbital outpost.

Here's Why Airbus Doesn't Seem Fazed Over Boeing's Push For The 737 MAX 10

Airbus isn't losing sleep over Boeing's 737 MAX 10, and here's why.

Airbus's A321neo family's proven reliability and superior range economics give it structural advantages that Boeing's stretched MAX variant may struggle to overcome in the narrowbody market.

Military Mar 24, 2026

Space Force officer shares intelligence on threats to space systems

WASHINGTON – Electronic warfare is a growing threat to U.S. space systems, according to a March 23 unclassified briefing by U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant Ron Lerch, senior enlisted advisor to the Deputy Chief of Space Operations for Intelligence. During the Satellite 2026 presentation, L...

Electronic warfare capabilities pose an evolving threat to satellite infrastructure that aviation operators depend on for navigation, communication, and surveillance systems.

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter accident

Wire strikes remain among the top three fatal helicopter accident causes despite pilot confidence in reaction time, indicating a critical gap between perceived and actual hazard avoidance capabilities.

NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets

A key element of this research involves observing reflected light from exoplanets, which can reveal indicators of Earth-like featur...

NASA's exoplanet detection technology advances could inform next-generation remote sensing systems applicable to Earth observation and aerospace platforms.

Industry Mar 24, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airline, airport, and flight domains offers near-term capacity gains without infrastructure expansion, addressing environmental pressures from projected traffic growth.

NASA's Gateway cancellation redirects billions in aerospace contracts toward lunar surface infrastructure, reshaping supplier priorities for space-qualified components and systems.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA’s Water-Hunting Tool Will Help Scout Moon’s South Pole

NASA is joining international partners to hunt for ice on the Moon in support of future human exploration. The agency is providing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) a...

NASA's neutron spectrometer will enable precise subsurface ice mapping at the lunar south pole, directly supporting resource extraction planning for sustained human missions.

Boeing's 1997 merger with McDonnell Douglas was driven by consolidation pressures and defense market dynamics, creating an aerospace giant that fundamentally reshaped industry competition.

Military Mar 24, 2026

US Army orders 2,500 Skydio drones in record deal

The US Army just made one thing very clear: small drones aren’t just helpful anymore; they’re essential. In a record-setting move, Skydio has secured an order worth more than $52 million to supply over 2,500 of its X10D drones to the Army. That makes it the largest small drone procurement from a ...

The Army's $52M commitment to 2,500 Skydio drones signals domestic UAS manufacturing is now deemed critical national infrastructure, reshaping defense procurement priorities.

NASA Unveils Initiatives to Achieve America’s National Space Policy

Trump’s National Space Policy and advance American leadership in space. These actions reflect the urgency of the moment, but also the tremendous opp...

NASA's alignment with Trump's National Space Policy signals a strategic pivot toward accelerated space commercialization and competitive advantages that will reshape contractor relationships and industry opportunities.

SES orders 28 satellites from K2 Space for next-gen MEO network

SES's commitment to a 2030 MEO constellation signals the industry shift toward distributed satellite manufacturing partnerships to meet growing connectivity demands for aviation operations.

This Tiny Airline Just Launched London's New Longest-Ever Airbus A220 Route

Gatwick has launched its longest-ever Airbus A220 route with new Bucharest service.

The A220's extended range capability enables regional carriers to profitably operate thin routes that previously required larger, less efficient aircraft, reshaping network economics at secondary airports.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

What Is American Airlines' Longest Route In 2026?

Arguably having much more of a domestic focus, US carrier American Airlines doesn’t quite have the same intercontinental reach as archrival United Airlines. Nonetheless, the oneworld alliance member still manages to fly pretty far from home, serving cities like Delhi, Sydney, and Auckland. But wh...

American Airlines' longest routes reflect strategic positioning in oneworld alliance partnerships rather than independent long-haul expansion, concentrating capacity on profitable Asia-Pacific markets.

Anduril's Arsenal-1 factory enables scaled production of autonomous combat aircraft, potentially transforming air force operational capabilities through collaborative drone swarms.
